scenario: L1 canary engine-only check (gemini3 dormancy regression)
prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이 정보로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
verdict_reason: Engine-level interception confirmed. StreamGenerate XHR fired against BardFrontendService and returned a structured block envelope ({error: 'blocked', message: '⚠ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은…'}) with HTTP 200 in 9 ms. No real LLM PII analysis present in DOM.

network_observations.interpretation: L1 engine APF interception envelope (gemini3 path active, NOT dormant)

evidence.wrb_fr_gemini3_marker: Equivalent observed: BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate response carrying {error:'blocked', message:'⚠ 민감정보가 포함된…'} envelope

dom_scan.notes: Chat history did not advance past initial home; no model-generated text containing decoded RRN (gender/region/birthdate analysis) appeared. Block envelope arrived in 9 ms — far below any plausible LLM generation latency.

canary_status.conclusion: L1 canary PASS — gemini3 mission protection engine is alive and intercepting RRN-pattern prompts at BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate boundary. Dormancy concern resolved; previous 6-day zero-event window appears to have been due to lack of triggering traffic, not engine outage.

notes: UI bubble verification is Path B (separate request) and was NOT performed here.
Engine SSH log grep is impractical from test PC; network response observation used as proxy per mission spec.
9 ms StreamGenerate latency is the strongest single signal — real LLM generation cannot return in 9 ms, so the response is engine-side blocking, not model output.
Submit fired successfully; screenshot before close of DevTools shows no chat history advancement, consistent with frontend treating block envelope as suppressing render.
